fixes_confirmed: Mobile dashboard now renders (height 3121px, fully scrollable) — strike 15 CLEARED
shortcut-toast no longer has inline styles (style=null confirmed via Puppeteer)
shortcut-toast now has aria-live='polite' and aria-label
still_broken: overflow:visible boundary bleed — SUNRISE element and character sprites still bleed outside office-floor container
Triple visibilitychange listeners — still 3 separate useEffect hooks at lines 850, 856, 1093; all have cleanup now but still unnecessary triple-registration
completionToast still uses a 16-property inline style block instead of a CSS class
Duplicate data-vibe CSS selectors — 5 pairs defined twice in Office.css (single-quote lines 91-95 then double-quote lines 97-115 — the first set is dead/overridden)
new_issues: Tab panel ARIA violation: conditional ternary rendering means inactive panel doesn't exist in DOM; aria-controls points to ghost element
polling-paused-pill is a <span> with onClick — not keyboard accessible, not a button
window.matchMedia() called inline during render (line 1194) — should be a media query hook
No H2 headings — Active Workers and Activity Log sections use div+class instead of semantic headings
window.location.hash set on tab click but never read on mount — broken deep linking

suggestions.title: Tab panel ARIA pattern broken — aria-controls targets ghost DOM element
suggestions.description: The two tabpanels are rendered with a ternary (tab==='office' ? <office-panel> : <dashboard-panel>). This means when Office is active, tabpanel-dashboard does NOT exist in the DOM, so aria-controls='tabpanel-dashboard' on the Dashboard tab button points to nothing. Screen readers silently fail. Fix: render BOTH panels always, hide the inactive one with the HTML `hidden` attribute instead of conditional rendering. e.g. <div role='tabpanel' id='tabpanel-office' hidden={tab !== 'office'}>. This is a WCAG 4.1.2 violation.

suggestions.title: office-floor overflow:visible still causing boundary bleed
suggestions.description: Office.css line 84: `.office-floor { overflow: visible; }` — confirmed visually in screenshot: the SUNRISE window element and character sprites (char--manager scrollWidth:104 vs offsetWidth:47; char--working 179 vs 40) visibly bleed outside the rounded office floor container. The intent is to allow character tooltips/speech bubbles to escape the container, but this is a blunt instrument. Fix: change overflow to `overflow: clip` (clips layout but allows positioned children to overflow), or use `overflow: visible` with a wrapper clip layer. If speech bubble overflow is intentional, add a comment and scope it precisely.

suggestions.title: polling-paused-pill is a <span> with onClick — keyboard inaccessible
suggestions.description: Line 1189-1194: `<span className='polling-paused-pill' onClick={onResume}>` — a span is not focusable and cannot be activated via keyboard. A user who tabs through the UI can never resume polling. Fix: change to `<button className='polling-paused-pill' onClick={onResume}>` and add `type='button'`. Also: remove `aria-live='polite'` from this element — live regions should be passive containers, not interactive controls. Move the live announcement to a separate sr-only span if needed.

suggestions.title: Duplicate data-vibe CSS selectors — dead first set in Office.css
suggestions.description: Office.css defines `office-floor[data-vibe='crushing']` (and the other 4 vibes) TWICE: lines 91-95 use single-quoted attribute selectors with only border-color and box-shadow, then lines 97-115 use double-quoted selectors with the same properties PLUS --grid-line. Because CSS cascades, the double-quote set at lines 97-115 wins. The single-quote set at lines 91-95 is entirely dead. Delete lines 91-95. This affects crushing, on-fire, in-flow, slow-day, and after-hours — that's 5 dead rule sets. While you're there, note that line 327 uses no-quote attribute syntax (.office-floor[data-vibe=crushing]) — pick one style and be consistent.

suggestions.title: completionToast still has 16-property inline style block — should be a CSS class
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1142-1163: the completionToast `<div>` has a massive style={{}} object covering position, bottom, left, transform, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, and animation. The dynamic parts (border color, box-shadow color) can be passed as CSS custom properties. Create `.completion-toast` in App.css for the static properties and use `style={{ '--toast-color': completionToast.color || '#6366f1' }}` for the dynamic ones. This was flagged in #58 and is still here.

suggestions.title: Triple visibilitychange listeners across three separate useEffect hooks
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 850-853 (handleVisibilityResume), 869-878 (handleVisibilityChange for interval), and 1093-1096 (handleVisibilityChange for document.title) — three separate useEffect hooks each independently add a visibilitychange listener. They all have proper cleanup now (improvement from #58), but there's no reason for three separate listeners. Consolidate into one useEffect that handles all three behaviors: resume polling, reset interval, and update title. Reduces listener overhead and makes the control flow dramatically easier to reason about.

suggestions.title: No semantic H2 headings — major sections use div+class instead
suggestions.description: The app has exactly one heading (H1: Bloberto's Office) and zero H2s. 'Active Workers (1)', 'Activity Log', and filter sections are all `.section-title` divs. Screen reader users navigating by heading jump straight from the H1 to nothing. Fix: change `.section-title` divs to `<h2>` elements and update the CSS selector accordingly (`.section-title` styles can still apply). The visual appearance won't change, but the document outline becomes meaningful.

suggestions.title: window.location.hash set on tab click but never read on mount — deep links silently broken
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1230: `onClick={() => { setTab('office'); window.location.hash = 'office' }}` and line 1243 same for dashboard. The hash IS written but there's no corresponding read on mount. A user who copies the URL with #dashboard and shares it will land on the Office tab. Either implement the read (add a useEffect on mount that reads window.location.hash and sets the initial tab accordingly) or remove the hash manipulation entirely. Half-baked routing is worse than none.

suggestions.title: window.matchMedia() called inline during render
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1194: `window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` is evaluated directly inside JSX during every render. This triggers a synchronous style resolution. Fix: hoist this to a module-level constant (`const IS_TOUCH = window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches`) or a `useMemo` / `useEffect` that updates on change (though pointer type rarely changes at runtime). At minimum, don't query it mid-render.

suggestions.title: Mobile: office tab label clips to 'Offic' when tooltip is active
suggestions.description: On mobile (375px), when a character speech bubble is positioned near the top of the viewport, it creates visual overlap with the tab toggle area, making the 'Office' tab button appear cropped to 'Offic'. The tab toggle needs `z-index` enforcement and/or the speech bubble positioning should be capped to prevent upward overflow past the office floor's top boundary. Consider `max-height` or `transform: translateY` clamping on speech bubbles.

suggestions.title: aria-live on shortcut-toast fires on mount with full text — noisy for screen readers
suggestions.description: The ShortcutToast component mounts with phase='pending' and immediately renders the shortcuts text into an aria-live='polite' region. This means screen readers announce the full shortcut string on every page load before the user has interacted with anything. The toast should start hidden from the accessibility tree (aria-hidden='true' while pending, removed when relevant) or the live region should only be activated on a state change, not initial mount.

suggestions.title: Scattered inline styles in worker card JSX — maintenance debt
suggestions.description: App.jsx still has multiple inline style props throughout the worker card: `style={{ flex: 1, paddingLeft: '0.75rem' }}` on worker-info (line 300), `style={{ marginRight: '0.35rem' }}` on activity icon (line 370), `style={{ opacity: 0.5, fontSize: '0.7rem', marginLeft: '0.35rem' }}` on back-time (line 372). None of these are dynamic. Move them to App.css / Office.css utility classes. This doesn't affect behavior but reduces JSX noise.

suggestions.title: Vibe sparkline (vibe-sparkline SVG) has overflow:visible but no visual clipping wrapper
suggestions.description: App.css line 187: `.vibe-sparkline { overflow: visible; }` — this allows the SVG sparkline to draw outside its bounding box (likely intentional for circle markers at endpoints). However there is no clipping parent, so on narrow stat cards the sparkline dots can render outside the card boundary. Wrap the SVG in a `position: relative; overflow: hidden` container, or clip only the SVG frame while keeping marker overflow via `overflow: visible` on the SVG element itself.
